reason: Entry when price breaks above confluence of intraday R1 and recent range highs with support levels below; exits on support loss or overbought conditions in trending regime.

Golden/Death cross strategy with 10-day SMA crossing 50-day SMA, requiring price confirmation above/below both MAs; enters long on bullish cross with price above both, exits on bearish cross; reverses for short signals.
Enter long when SMA 10 crosses above SMA 50, exit when it crosses below; enter short on reverse crossover, filtered to TRENDING regime.
Enter long on the fifth up trading day of January (if all five are positive) and hold until year-end; exit conditions default to end-of-year as AUTHOR_SILENT implies hold entire year.
Uses sma_50 (midpoint of 45-75 day range) for trend-following entries/exits with reversals as exit signals; context-dependent direction generates both long and short rules for trending regimes.
Enter long when price crosses above a moving average (trend turn up); exit when trend reverses (price crosses below MA).
Enters long after a lower opening in an uptrend only when the low occurs before the high during the day, ruled out by dominant upper wick which signals high-first reversal pattern.
Enter long only after trend signal (ADX >25) confirms and price closes below lower Bollinger Band; exit when trend reverses (ADX weakens below 20).
SMA crossover strategy using sma_10 (short) and sma_50 (long) with entries on level crossings and exits on reversals in trending markets.
Enter when trend is established (ADX>25), gap occurs (open/close reversal), price retraces to pivot support/resistance, and trend resumes (price crosses pivot); exit when trend weakens (ADX<20).

Enter long when price consolidates above daily pivot, aligns with 20MA and 50MA near weekly pivot level, and breaks above consolidation resistance (approximated as R1); exit on moving average breakdown.
Buy when price crosses above or trades above the 20-period SMA in a trending regime; exit when price closes below the 20MA support line.
Signal prohibits shorting at gap-down opens after breakdowns; instead defines a long-only strategy waiting for pullback entries in trending markets, with short entries explicitly disabled.

Entry requires price to cross the 20-day SMA with penetration magnitude exceeding one ATR period, signaling trend initiation; exit on SMA reversal; context-dependent direction based on penetration direction.
Entry triggers when price crosses above MA (sma_20 as default) with support confirmation; exit on MA break below; holds up to 8 candles to capture sustained trending move as per signal specification.
Buy options when 20-day historical volatility is near historical lows with neutral price momentum (RSI 40-60), exiting when realized volatility expands significantly above the implied level.
Buy when stock opens below pivot, holds above S1 support, and closes higher on intraday retracement; exit if price breaks below S1 support.
Detects trend reversal via failure patterns (springs/upthrusts) at extremes using wick structure, RSI/MACD weakness, and exits on momentum surge beyond prior extreme with volume confirmation.

Entry shorts when price crosses below 20MA in a confirmed downtrend (20MA below 50MA) and exits on reversal above 20MA; the 'MA caps subsequent rises' and 'price moves towards MA from above' conditions are captured by the crossbelow and trend confirmation logic.
Buy when 10-day SMA is below yesterday's close AND 5-day stochastic K exceeds 50; exit when stochastic K reverses below 50 threshold.
Entry when MACD histogram crosses above zero in trending regimes; exit when histogram returns to or falls below zero.

Volume spike identified when current volume is at least 2x the 20-day average (proxy for observation period); direction is context-dependent (determined by price action), so both long and short entries apply to the same spike condition; exit after 5 days as a default hold period reflecting the signal's association with significant price move endpoints.

Double bottom pattern entry when price consolidates near lows with declining volume confirmation; exit on reversal below formation or after 20 days; related security confirmation and breakout alternative cannot be mechanically tested without multi-asset correlation data.

Volume spike detected when current volume exceeds 5-day lagged average by multiplier; neutral direction triggers both long/short entries; exit after spike development window (3 days max) or on stop loss.
Entry when price consolidates above weekly pivot and breaks above weekly R1 while approaching both 20MA and 50MA; exit when price closes below pivot level.
Enter long when price closes or opens above the central pivot (pivot column); exit when price closes back below pivot; filter for trending regimes only.
Mean reversion fade trades triggered when price extends beyond Bollinger Bands with RSI confirmation and elevated volume, exiting on mean reversion or time decay.
Enters LONG when a small red candle (body < ATR_14) has a longer lower shadow (lower_wick > upper_wick) indicating bullish reversal; exits on upper Bollinger Band cross as reversal signal.
Buy when price crosses above or stays above 5-day SMA (trend confirmation), exit when price crosses below SMA; AUTHOR_SILENT exit replaced with mean-reversion reversal logic.

Entry requires a giant candle in trend direction with volume confirmation; exit triggers when countertrend retracement invalidates the setup by exceeding the 50% threshold of the prior giant candle's range.
SMA(10) crossing SMA(20) with price confirmation and volume triggers context-dependent long/short entries; reversal of crossover signals exits.

Entry triggers when current true_range exceeds the 2-period average true range (approximated by atr_14 as the closest available volatility measure); direction is NEUTRAL so both long and short entries apply; exit uses default 5-day hold since AUTHOR_SILENT.

Long entries when RSI reaches overbought (≥70) with volume confirmation; short entries when RSI reaches oversold (≤30) with volume confirmation; exit on momentum mean reversion or price breaking support/resistance levels.
Enter long/short when rolling mean of log_returns crosses above/below zero with 1-bar forward shift, exit when signal reverses sign.
Enter long/short when rolling mean of log returns crosses above/below zero with confirmation persistence, exit on reversal crossover.
Enter long when MACD penetrates below Bollinger lower band and crosses above its signal line; exit when MACD crosses below signal line in trending regime.
Enters on trend confirmation (ADX, price vs MA) plus pullback signal (wick > body) with close reversal back into trend direction; exits on trend structure break (price crosses MA or ADX weakens).

Entry triggers on confirmed trend (ADX>=25) with pullback compression (range < ATR and reduced body %) and low volume conviction; exits when pullback structure breaks symmetry (range expands) or trend reversal occurs (price crosses MA).
Price crossing above/below moving average triggers trend reversals; exits occur on counter-crossovers in a trending regime.

Entry triggers when price forms reversal at moving average (20MA primary), with exits on MA crossovers in opposite direction; applicable to both trending up (long entries at MA support) and trending down (short entries at MA resistance) scenarios.
Buy when SMA slope rises (price above MA), sell when slope falls (price below MA), with reversal exits and trending regime filter.
Price crossovers of the 20-day SMA generate bidirectional trend-following signals; exit conditions reverse the entry signals since exit logic is author-silent.
Entry when price crosses above the central pivot (daily); exit when price crosses back below pivot due to AUTHOR_SILENT; filtered to trending regime only.
Buy on close crossing above the pivot point (calculated with 5-day lookback); sell on close crossing below the pivot point, with reversals triggering opposite positions.
Entry triggered when price bounces from 50% retracement (pivot level) and resumes uptrend above MA; exit on trend reversal or support break.
Enter long when price touches/breaches lower Bollinger Band (2 SD) signaling end of sell-off in ranging regimes; exit when price crosses back above the band middle.
Entry when intraday open exceeds daily pivot level (bullish signal); exit on close below pivot or after 1 day hold as default for daily-pivot intraday trades.
8/18-period moving average crossover system using EMA equivalents (5/20 closest match) to capture trend entries when fast crosses slow, with reversal exits and trending regime filter.
Enters longs at upper range extremes and shorts at lower extremes in ranging markets (low ADX), exits when price reaches opposite Bollinger Band extreme, with stop loss just below entry.
Faster trendline (EMA 10) crossing long-term trendline (EMA 50) generates directional entries; opposite crossover triggers exits, suitable for trending regimes.
Entry triggers on uptrend confirmation via rising 20MA, price crossing above weekly pivot and near R1 resistance; exit on MA/pivot breakdown or trend reversal.

Fade price extensions beyond Keltner channel boundaries (approximated by Bollinger Bands with 2.0 ATR multiplier equivalent) by entering counter-trend when price closes beyond bands and exiting at midline, with stop loss at 2× ATR from entry.
Enter long when a rejection candle forms at support in an uptrend and price breaks above rejection high; exit on stop loss below recent low or by ATR-based trailing stop.
Buy on 1.96% decline and sell on 2.24% rise following large moves, with fixed 2-day holding period using intraday returns as the volatility trigger.

Buy when an uptrend (ADX > 25, price > SMA20) shows a price reversal pattern (large lower wick relative to body) indicating buyers stepping in; exit when trend breaks below SMA20 or after 3-day cycle completes.
Enter when price and MACD histogram diverge (price making higher/lower highs while momentum weakens), exit when divergence resolves via MACD crossing zero or after 5 days.
SMA10/SMA20 crossover strategy: buy on upward cross with price confirmation, sell/exit on downward cross in trending markets.
Entry when market opens above pivot/R1 and pulls back to support at pivot; exit on close reaching R2 or higher in trending regime.

Entry triggers when month-end price is near the low of the 5-year range (≤8.1% stochastic level); seasonal long position held for ~20 trading days with default stop-loss; exit conditions unspecified by author so reasonable hold period applied.
Buy when price closes in upper 20% of range (price_pos_20 >= 0.8) and shows continuation above SMA, exit after 5 days or on stop-loss trigger.
Buy/sell when price crosses above/below faster MA (sma_10) while it remains below/above slower MA (sma_50) with rising volume; exit when long-term MA (sma_200) conflicts with the trend signal.
Enter long when price stays above S1 support in uptrends, enter short when price stays below R1 resistance in downtrends; exit when price breaches these levels in the opposite direction.

Enters on MACD histogram sign reversals after consolidation near exhaustion zones (price beyond 2σ from 20-MA) and exits on momentum reversal or 3x risk profit target, context-dependent long/short for trending/high-vol regimes.
Price crossovers of an exponential smoothing trendline (EMA_20) trigger entries and reversals; exits occur when price crosses back through the trendline in the opposite direction.

Enter long when uptrend confirmed (ADX, price above SMA50, gap/volatility spike detected) and retracement occurs (price_pos_20); enter short when downtrend confirmed; exit on trend reversal (price crossing moving average).

Pullback trade entry on confirmed trend with confirmation from swing points (pivot levels); exits on price reaching first profit target zone near pivot/resistance-support or trend reversal without momentum continuation.
Enter when intraday and daily trends align (confirmed by ADX > 25 and price position relative to key moving averages across multiple timeframes), exit on trend reversal on the daily chart.

Detects narrow pivot ranges (via Bollinger Bandwidth as CPR proxy) over a 3-day lookback, enters on breakouts above/below pivot support/resistance levels, and exits when price reverts toward central pivot or after 5 days.

Bull spread entry requires confirmation that nearby prices are outperforming (price above MA) in a confirmed uptrend (ADX > 25); exit when trend strength weakens (ADX < 20) or reversal conditions trigger.

Buys pullbacks to pivot/R1 after up-opens for uptrend continuation to R2/R3; sells pullbacks to pivot/S1 after down-opens for downtrend continuation to S2/S3, using available pivot levels and price action.
Entry triggers on price breaking out above R1 (long) or below S1 (short); exit when price rejects and drifts back toward the central pivot point.
Entry triggers on today's true_range exceeding the 14-day ATR (proxy for avg prior N-day true range); exit on volatility contraction; applied to 2-bar reversals in high-volatility regimes.
Continuation trade on failed reversals: enter when price reverses above/below pivot with volume and momentum confirmation; exit on momentum fade or protective stops at swing levels.
Enter long/short on 10-period SMA crossing above/below 20-period SMA (1:2 ratio); exit on opposite cross in trending regimes.
Fast SMA (sma_10) crossing above/below slow SMA (sma_20) triggers long/short entries with reversal-based exits; default 10/20 periods used as closest standard moving average pair for trend-following.
Trend-following system using ADX for trend establishment and ATR-based reversal threshold (box size) as exit trigger when price changes direction by the minimum move.
Mean reversion strategy on overbought/oversold conditions (RSI, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic) with exit at mean and 10-day max hold period, targeting ranging regimes.
Entry signals when daily open crosses above/below weekly pivot levels; exit when price reverses back through pivot or after 5 days; filtered to trending regimes only.
Entry when CPR transitions from resistance to support and price closes above it in an established uptrend; exit when price falls back below pivot support.
Enters on directional price movement (above/below SMA-20) with elevated volume and ADX confirmation; exits on reversal, loss of directional pressure (low body%), or volume drying up.
Entry triggers on sustained trend with reversal confirmation; exits on 38.2% Fibonacci retracement profit target with 20-day hold default and 2% stop-loss.
Trade mean-reversion at range boundaries after failed breakouts; entry when price reverts inside range in low-ADX environment, exit on momentum reversal or MA cross.
Entry triggers when price retraces to support at previous close (above central pivot); exits on first resistance break or 2% stop-loss due to AUTHOR_SILENT conditions.
Enter long/short based on sign of rolling mean log returns (momentum); exit when momentum reverses or holding period (5 bars default) expires.
Wide-ranging day (range > ATR) with close near extreme (price_pos near 0 or 1) triggers directional entry; exit on reversal signal or hold default.
Intraday reversion buy when price dips below pivot but holds above S1 support and shows upward momentum from the low.
Pivot-based context-dependent bias signal: go long when price holds above pivot (support at daily pivot low), go short when price breaks below pivot range, exit on reversal through pivot levels.
Enters long/short on confirmed trend (ADX > 25) plus stochastic K/D crossover, with short entries requiring additional 80% overbought condition; exits on opposing stochastic crossover signals.
Entry requires price reversal pattern confirmation via RSI and MACD agreement; exit triggers on support/resistance breach or indicator mean reversion in TRENDING regimes only.
Buy/sell on n-day price momentum direction changes using returns (price change) as the primary indicator; reversal conditions exit trades when momentum flips sign.
Rolling average of returns generates context-dependent momentum signals; go long when returns average positive, short when negative, exit on zero-crossover.
Multi-horizon momentum strategy that enters long/short based on rolling mean sign of log returns across 4 lookback periods, exits when the 15-bar rolling sign flips, and operates in trending regimes.
Buy instruments with positive 12-month returns and sell with negative returns; exit on sign reversal or after 180-day max holding period within 3-12 month range.

Exit trend trades when MACD crosses below/above its signal line, signaling sharp counter-trend momentum that breaks established trend pattern; entry requires confirmed trend via price/MA alignment and strong ADX.
Entry on a single minor opposing candle (small body) during strong trend (ADX>=25), exiting when trend weakens (ADX<20), ignoring negligible retracements in strong momentum.
Doji/small-body candles signal market indecision in ranging regimes; entry when body and wicks are minimal, exit when body expands or trend strength emerges.

Enter long when price closes above pivot and resistance level 1, enter short when price closes below pivot and support level 1; exit when price crosses back through the central pivot point indicating rejection of the new value level.

Entry triggers when price breaks simultaneously beyond both the previous day's support/resistance levels (r1/s1) and the central pivot level, expecting amplified directional moves; exit on pivot reversal.
Buy on 3-day momentum (RSI-7 proxy) below 30; exit next open if trade is profitable at day-end close and next open moves favorably.

Entry requires established trend (ADX ≥25 confirms trending regime) plus failure to exceed previous pivot high/low (with-trend leg failure); exit on bar close that fails to make new extreme, with no re-entry until new extreme is made.
Enter long when uptrend is confirmed (ADX > 25), price stays above pivot/S1 support levels; exit when price closes below S1 support.

Enter long on higher highs/lows confirmation with stop beyond prior support; enter short on lower lows/highs confirmation with stop beyond prior resistance; ADX confirms trending regime; exits trigger at pivot reversals.

Entry on breakout through support/resistance (pivot) with confirmation that price holds above/below level without violation; exit on close back through breakout level with tight stop-loss to manage violent reversals in trending regimes.
Enter long when most recent log return is positive, enter short when negative; exit when sign reverses or threshold is not met next period.

Uses ADX (longer-term trend detector) combined with stochastic K crossings (oversold <20 for long, overbought >80 for short) to time entries within trending regimes, with reversal exits when stochastic reaches opposite extreme.
Long entry when price breaks above pivot resistance (r1); short entry when price breaks below pivot support (s1); exit when price reverses back through the broken level.

Enters when ADX confirms trend strength and price is within volatility-adjusted bands (approximated via Bollinger Bands at 2.25σ equivalent); exits when price closes beyond outer band, signaling exhaustion or breakout.
Buy when price crosses above the pivot point in a trending regime; exit when price reverses below pivot (AUTHOR_SILENT interpreted as trend-reversal exit).

Volume-based trend confirmation using vol_ratio_20 (20-day volume average proxy) with 20% threshold for entry and reversion to baseline for exit; direction context-dependent on market regime (TRENDING).

Enter long when price dips below support (previous close/morning low proxy via S1 pivot) then recovers in an uptrend; exit on trend reversal or after 5 days with 2% stop loss and 0.8% profit target matching the 60-80 point expected move.
Buy/long on price crossing above previous pivot high (R1), sell/exit on break below previous pivot low (S1); uses available pivot columns for trend-following entry/exit logic.
Buy/sell when price crosses innermost Bollinger Bands (slightly oversold/overbought), exit when price returns to middle band (mean); filters for ranging regime.
CPR reversion strategy enters long after falling markets close above pivot (bullish reversal) and short after rising markets close below pivot (bearish reversal), with reversal-based exits.
Enter when volume ratio exceeds 20-day average and increased volatility is present; exit when volume contracts below average, favoring high-volume regimes and avoiding midday low-volume periods.
Context-dependent pullback entry into established trends with tight flag structures, exiting at trend breaks or structural pivot violations.
